text.title: Business Model Research Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Business Model Research. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.root-cause: Pattern-match to aspirational claims; LLM seeded by founder language.
antipattern.fix: Cap take-rate at 5-15% unless 10-K evidence supports higher; Etsy 6.5%, Airbnb 14%, Upwork 10% are the comparables.

antipattern.symptom: Cost Structure block reuses revenue numbers; LTV inflated.
antipattern.fix: Source gross margin from a comparable 10-K or run an explicit COGS breakdown; AI-heavy SaaS often runs 30-50% margin, not 80%.

antipattern.symptom: Model claims 15% freemium conversion with no citation.
antipattern.fix: Floor at 2% consumer / 5% B2B; require an empirical citation or Soft-tag the cell.

antipattern.symptom: Same monthly churn applied for 36 months.
antipattern.root-cause: Author used the simplest formula they remembered.
antipattern.fix: Model month-1 churn 3-10x month-12; cohort-variable churn before any LTV cell renders.
